about.title: Career changer with a plan.
about.text: I actually have a background in sales, but recently decided to fully commit to coding. That's why I'm currently doing a one-year fullstack bootcamp. I just really enjoy turning ideas into code and having a working project in front of me at the end of the day. That's exactly why I love diving into new topics and learning something new every single day, even if things turn out to be more complicated than they seem.
about.location: Emden, East Frisia. Working remote from here.

join.name: Join
join.description: A typical Kanban board for task management. You can create tasks, assign them to people and move them from 'To Do' to 'Done' via drag and drop.
join.learnings: The biggest challenge was actually working with four people on the same codebase. You quickly realize how important clear Git workflows are. Towards the end, I took some time to properly clean up and structure the code.
join.umsetzung: I mainly took care of the summary dashboard. I also built the drag and drop logic for the board and created the required legal pages.

dabubble.name: DABubble
dabubble.description: A sort of Slack clone for teams. You can chat in channels or directly with each other and reply to messages in threads – all in real time, of course.
dabubble.learnings: I built the entire chat interface. It got really tricky when I had to make sure that the sidebar, chat area, and open threads always stayed in sync without interfering with each other when new messages arrived.
dabubble.umsetzung: We used Angular for the frontend and hooked up Supabase as the database. The chat relies on Supabase's real-time subscriptions. Aside from that, I also built the auth, profiles, and a search feature.

el_pollo_loco.name: El Pollo Loco
el_pollo_loco.description: A small jump and run game that I wrote entirely in plain JavaScript. The main character collects coins and salsa bottles to defeat a boss enemy at the end.
el_pollo_loco.learnings: Collision detection definitely gave me the biggest headache. When exactly do you jump on an enemy, and when do you take damage yourself? Encapsulating all of this flawlessly in objects and classes was a great exercise.
el_pollo_loco.umsetzung: Everything was built from scratch in vanilla JavaScript. It features a custom game loop, object hierarchies for the enemies, and some basic state management for the game phases.

pokedex.name: Pokedex
pokedex.description: A kind of encyclopedia for Pokémon. The data is pulled live from the PokeAPI. You can scroll through all Pokémon, filter them, and view their exact stats.
pokedex.learnings: Since the API requires an enormous amount of individual requests for certain things, the page was initially very slow. I had to learn how to smartly batch requests and optimize loading times.
pokedex.umsetzung: Mostly asynchronous JavaScript (Fetch) to retrieve the data, combined with search and filter logic for the frontend.

portfolio.name: Portfolio
portfolio.description: A practice project from the bootcamp. We were given a finished Figma design, and I tried to recreate it as closely as possible using Angular – including all the little animations that were in the mockup.
portfolio.learnings: Because the design included so many animations, I had to be careful to keep the page from lagging. I spent quite a bit of time figuring out how to use CSS keyframes efficiently without overloading the browser.
portfolio.umsetzung: Just classic Angular with SCSS. For the animations, I made sure to extract them into reusable CSS classes.

contact.title: Let's talk.
contact.subtitle: Open to an internship, working student position or a junior role, remote, hybrid or on-site in East Frisia.

cv.name: Jens Baumann
cv.profile_text: Coding has always fascinated me. After a few years in a different career path, I finally decided to make the jump into software development. I bring a lot of real-world experience in dealing with people and projects, I pick up new technologies quickly, and I really care about writing code that isn't just functional, but also clean and maintainable.
cv.exp_agency_text: Six months in sales at a social media agency. A valuable look behind the scenes of agency life and the world of digital marketing.
cv.exp_lead_text: Leading the telecommunications department with responsibility for the team, consulting and sales. Here I developed communication skills, structure and a solution-oriented mindset that benefit me in development today.
cv.edu_da_text: 12-month intensive training program in modern web development at the Developer Akademie: I have successfully completed the first 6 months focusing on frontend (JavaScript, Angular, TypeScript) and have now started with backend development. Practical learning through real-world projects and group work.
cv.edu_telekom_text: Apprenticeship focused on sales and customer consulting in the T-Shop (Wilhelmshaven and Emden). Six months of which at DTKS in Leer (customer service for full-provider switchers).

legal.title: Legal Notice
legal.liability_content_text: As a service provider, I am responsible for my own content on these pages in accordance with general laws pursuant to § 7 (1) DDG. According to §§ 8 to 10 DDG, however, I am not obliged to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity. Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general law remain unaffected. Liability in this respect is only possible from the point in time at which knowledge of a specific infringement of the law becomes known. Upon becoming aware of corresponding legal violations, I will remove this content immediately.
legal.liability_links_text: My website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content I have no influence. Therefore, I cannot accept any liability for this external content. The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for their content. The linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognisable at the time of linking. However, permanent monitoring of the content of the linked pages is not reasonable without concrete evidence of an infringement. Upon becoming aware of legal violations, I will remove such links immediately.
legal.copyright_text: The content and works created by the site operator on these pages are subject to German copyright law. Duplication, processing, distribution and any kind of exploitation outside the limits of copyright require the written consent of the respective author or creator. Downloads and copies of this site are only permitted for private, non-commercial use. Insofar as the content on this site was not created by the operator, the copyrights of third parties are respected. Should you nevertheless become aware of a copyright infringement, please inform me accordingly. Upon becoming aware of legal violations, I will remove such content immediately.
legal.credits_text: The Tux mascot was created by Larry Ewing using The GIMP (lewing@isc.tamu.edu). All other logos and trademarks shown are the property of their respective owners and are used solely to identify the respective technologies.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1_text: The following information provides a simple overview of what happens to your personal data when you visit this website. Personal data is any data with which you can be personally identified. This website does not use cookies for tracking, does not use any analytics or advertising services, and does not create user profiles.
privacy.s2_intro: The party responsible for data processing on this website is:
privacy.s3_text: This website is hosted by Hetzner Online GmbH, Industriestr. 25, 91710 Gunzenhausen, Germany ("Hetzner"). When you visit this website, Hetzner processes various information, including your IP address, in order to deliver the website. The legal basis is Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR; I have a legitimate interest in the most reliable and secure presentation of my website. A data processing agreement (DPA) pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR has been concluded with Hetzner.
privacy.s4_intro: The hosting provider automatically collects and stores information in so-called server log files, which your browser automatically transmits. These are:
privacy.s4_li4: host name of the accessing computer
privacy.s4_outro: This data is not merged with other data sources. It is collected on the basis of Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR for the purpose of ensuring a technically error-free presentation and the optimisation of the website.
privacy.s5_title: 5. Contact form and contact by email
privacy.s5_p1: If you send me an enquiry via the contact form, the data you enter in the form (name, email address and your message) will be transmitted to a script on the server and forwarded to my email address (info@jensbaumann.com) for the purpose of processing your enquiry. I do not pass this data on without your consent.
privacy.s5_p2: The processing of this data is based on Art. 6 (1) (a) GDPR (consent, which you grant via the checkbox) and Art. 6 (1) (b) GDPR if your request is related to the performance of a contract or pre-contractual measures. The data you enter will remain with me until you ask me to delete it, revoke your consent, or the purpose for data storage no longer applies (e.g. after your request has been processed). Mandatory legal provisions – in particular retention periods – remain unaffected.
privacy.s7_text: This website stores your preferences for the color scheme (light, dark or system) and the selected language in your browser's local storage (a functional storage technology, not a tracking cookie). This data remains on your device, is not transmitted to me, and can be deleted at any time via your browser settings. The legal basis is Art. 6 (1) (f) GDPR (legitimate interest in a user-friendly presentation of the website).
privacy.s8_text: For security reasons and to protect the transmission of confidential content, this site uses SSL/TLS encryption. You can recognise an encrypted connection by the "https://" in your browser's address bar and the lock symbol.
privacy.s9_intro: You have the following rights at any time:
privacy.s9_li1: the right to information about your stored data (Art. 15 GDPR)
privacy.s9_li2: the right to rectification of incorrect data (Art. 16 GDPR)
privacy.s9_li4: the right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
privacy.s9_li5: the right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)
privacy.s9_li6: the right to object to processing (Art. 21 GDPR)
privacy.s9_li7: the right to withdraw consent given at any time (Art. 7 (3) GDPR)
privacy.s9_li8: the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority (Art. 77 GDPR)
privacy.s9_outro: To exercise these rights, you can contact me at any time using the contact details in section 2.
